udon-noodles.name: Udon Noodles

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 1 tbsp. plus  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Stir 2 Tbsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 Tbsp. plus ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt into ¾ cup plus 2 Tbsp. (198 g) water in a small bowl until dissolved. Place 3¼ cups (406 g) all-purpose flour in a large bowl and drizzle salt water evenly over. Stir with a spoon or chopsticks until water is absorbed and shaggy clumps form. Knead dough firmly in bowl until a tight, mostly smooth dough forms, 8–10 minutes (there may be some dry bits but there shouldn’t be any large patches of dry flour). Form dough into a ball and place inside a 2-gal. resealable plastic bag (or, split dough in half and place each in a 1-gal. resealable plastic bag). Seal bag, pressing out excess air and let rest 30 minutes.

Place bag with dough between 2 kitchen towels and place on the floor. Using your heels, step on dough to flatten into a thin disk (hold on to the counter so you don’t slip). Open bag and fold dough into quarters, like a piece of paper. Seal bag, pressing out air, and place back between kitchen towels. Step on dough until flat. Repeat process of folding and stepping 3 more times (5 total). The dough will feel noticeably softer at the end. Seal bag tightly and let dough rest at least 2 hours and up to 12 hours. (If storing longer than 2 hours, chill and bring to room temperature before using).
Flatten dough on a surface to ½" thick. Roll dough out to a 12x10" rectangle about ⅛" thick. For the most even noodles, trim the edges of the dough to make a neat rectangle.

Lightly dust dough with potato starch or cornstarch and fold in half. Using a sharp knife or a bench scraper, cut dough into ⅛"-wide noodles. Shake noodles to detangle, dusting with more potato starch as needed to prevent sticking (try to use less rather than more starch here).
Cook noodles in a large pot of boiling water (no need to salt) until al dente, 8–10 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold running water to remove excess starch and make noodles bouncy. Use immediately.

upside-down-peach-pie-argiro.name: Upside-down Peach Pie

ingredients.item: 1 cup flour for all uses sieved
1/2 pack (125 gr) butter at room temperature
1 cup flour for all uses sieved
1/2 pack (125 gr) butter at room temperature

instructions: We'll use small ring form or small 23 cm pan.
We butter it and spread parchment paper on the base.
Set half the finely chopped apricots in the baking pan.
Clean the fresh peaches (be careful not to be too made because they will make a lot of liquids in the baking).
Remove the peel and the pit and cut them into 6 or 8 slices.
Mix the sugar and cinnamon in a bowl and keep 2 tablespoons of the mixture separate.
Add the fresh peaches and mix to cover with cinnamon sugar.
Put them on the pan radially so we can cover it all.
Sprinkle with the rest of the cinnamon sugar.
Beat the butter with the two kids of sugar with a hand mixer for 2–3–1.
Add the eggs, vanilla, yogurt, cognac.
Finally, add the remaining chopped dried apricots.
Lower the speed and add the flour and salt.
We don't beat the mixture much.
We can incorporate flour with a spoon or spatula.
Then pour the mixture onto the peaches.
